notificationChannels.railBody: These channels receive outbound notifications when a new incident is created. They are separate from on-call escalation (email, SMS, voice to individual users).
notificationChannels.railBullet1: You choose which lifecycle steps notify this channel (created, acknowledged, resolved).
notificationChannels.railBullet2: Optional filters: minimum severity and specific services.
notificationChannels.railBullet3: Use Test to verify credentials before relying on production traffic.

notificationChannels.webhookPayloadHint: Your endpoint receives POST or PUT with Content-Type application/json. Optional header: X-Webhook-Secret when you set a secret.

services.apiKeyShowOnceWarn: Copy this API key now — it will not be shown again. Add it as the ?apiKey= query parameter on the webhook URL.
services.showHowToCall: Show how to call this webhook
services.howToCallBody: Send a POST request to the URL above. The apiKey query parameter authenticates the caller; without it the request is rejected.
services.howToHmacBody: Compute HMAC-SHA256 of the raw request body using the signature secret you configured, then send it as a hex string in the header above (prefixed with sha256=).
services.hmacExplain: Lets your sender prove the request hasn't been tampered with: you HMAC-SHA256 the request body with this secret and pass the hex digest in the header. Callu re-computes it on receive and rejects mismatched requests. The secret is shown once at creation — copy it before navigating away.
services.enableListeningHint: Enable listening mode below to start receiving webhooks
services.listeningModeDescription: When enabled, incoming webhooks are captured and logged without triggering incidents. Use this to learn the payload structure.

legal.openSourceFooter: Callu is open-source software (MIT License). Source code and full license terms are available at the project repository.
legal.terms.title: Terms of Service
legal.terms.intro: Callu is open-source incident management software distributed under the MIT License. These terms cover the relationship between you (the operator) running this self-hosted Callu instance and Callu.app (the upstream software provider). They do not govern the relationship between the operator and the end users of this instance — that's between you and them.
legal.terms.softwareLicenseBody: Callu is licensed under the MIT License. You may use, copy, modify, merge, publish, distribute, sublicense, and sell copies of the software subject to the conditions in the LICENSE file shipped with this distribution. The MIT License includes a complete copyright notice and disclaimer of warranty.
legal.terms.operatorResponsibilityBody: You operate this Callu instance on infrastructure you control. As the operator, you are solely responsible for:
legal.terms.operatorBullet1: Configuring, securing, and maintaining the deployment (database, network, secrets, TLS, backups).
legal.terms.operatorBullet2: Compliance with all laws and regulations that apply to your jurisdiction and your users (GDPR, CCPA, telecommunications law, employment law, etc.).
legal.terms.operatorBullet3: Any communications sent from this instance via SMTP, SMS, or voice — including consent, throttling, and content compliance (e.g. STIR/SHAKEN, anti-spam laws).
legal.terms.operatorBullet4: Publishing your own Terms of Service and Privacy Policy to the end users of this instance if your local law requires it.
legal.terms.noWarrantyBody: The software is provided "AS IS", without warranty of any kind, express or implied, including but not limited to the warranties of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose and non-infringement. In no event shall the authors or copyright holders be liable for any claim, damages or other liability arising from the software or its use.
legal.terms.limitationBody: To the maximum extent permitted by law, in no event will the Callu.app authors, contributors, or copyright holders be liable for any direct, indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages, including loss of profits, revenue, data, or business interruption, arising out of or in connection with your use of the software.
legal.terms.thirdPartyBody: Callu can integrate with third-party services (Voximplant for voice calls, SMTP providers for email, RabbitMQ, Redis, PostgreSQL). When you configure these integrations, you accept the respective terms and privacy policies of those providers. Callu.app has no relationship with the third-party services you choose to use.
legal.terms.acceptableUseBody: You agree not to use the software for any unlawful purpose, to harass or harm others, to send unsolicited communications, or to violate applicable telecommunications and privacy laws. The Callu.app authors disclaim responsibility for any operator's misuse of the software.
legal.terms.changesBody: These terms apply to the version of Callu shipped with this distribution. Future versions of the software may ship with updated terms. The version history is available in the project repository.
legal.terms.contactBody: Questions about the software itself (bugs, features, security disclosures) belong in the project repository. Questions about how this specific instance is operated belong with the operator of this deployment.
legal.privacy.title: Privacy Policy
legal.privacy.intro: Callu is self-hosted software. This page describes the categories of personal data that Callu processes by design, so the operator of this instance can decide how to disclose them to their own users. Callu.app (the upstream software provider) does not collect any data from this instance — only the operator can see it.
legal.privacy.controllerBody: The operator running this Callu instance is the data controller (in GDPR terms). Callu.app authored the software but has no operational access to it, no telemetry, and no log feed from your deployment. If you are a user of this instance and want to know who that operator is, ask the admin who invited you.
legal.privacy.dataCollectedBody: Callu stores the following categories of data in the operator's database:
legal.privacy.dataAccountBody: email, display name, hashed password, optional phone number, optional timezone and locale, last-login timestamp.
legal.privacy.dataIncidentBody: title, description, severity, timestamps, the user who acknowledged or resolved, free-form notes, postmortems.
legal.privacy.dataContactBody: phone numbers used for SMS and voice paging, email addresses used for notifications.
legal.privacy.dataAuditBody: who did what, when — for security and incident post-mortems. Cannot be edited by end users.
legal.privacy.dataAuthBody: active refresh tokens (hashed), security stamps. Refresh tokens are revoked on password change, role change, or user removal.
legal.privacy.noTelemetryBody: Callu does not phone home. No usage analytics, crash reports, or telemetry are sent to Callu.app or any third party by default. The only outbound network traffic Callu generates is to services the operator explicitly configures (SMTP, SMS provider, voice provider, webhook destinations).
legal.privacy.thirdPartyBody: When the operator configures these integrations, the relevant data leaves the Callu deployment and is processed by the configured provider under that provider's own privacy terms:
legal.privacy.thirdPartyBullet1: SMTP provider — recipient email address, subject line, and body of notification emails.
legal.privacy.thirdPartyBullet2: Voice / SMS provider (e.g. Voximplant) — recipient phone number, call metadata (duration, ack DTMF), spoken/SMS message text.
legal.privacy.thirdPartyBullet3: Outbound webhook targets — incident metadata as configured in the webhook template.
legal.privacy.retentionBody: Retention is set by the operator. Callu does not automatically delete incidents, notes, or audit logs — that decision belongs to the operator's policy. User accounts are soft-deleted on removal so the audit trail stays intact; an explicit "forget me" hard delete is the operator's responsibility under applicable law.
legal.privacy.securityBody: SMTP and SIP passwords are encrypted at rest using ASP.NET Core Data Protection. Refresh tokens are stored as SHA-256 hashes. JWT access tokens carry a SecurityStamp claim that invalidates on any credential or role event. The operator is responsible for the security posture of the host (TLS termination, secrets management, OS patching, network isolation).
legal.privacy.userRightsHeading: 7. Your rights as an end user
legal.privacy.userRightsBody: Rights to access, rectify, port, or erase your personal data are honoured by the operator of this instance, not by Callu.app. Contact the operator (the admin who invited you) to exercise those rights.
legal.privacy.cookiesBody: Callu uses a single HttpOnly, SameSite=Strict refresh-token cookie scoped to /api/v1/auth. No tracking cookies, no analytics cookies, no third-party cookies. The cookie is deleted on logout.
legal.privacy.changesBody: This policy describes the behaviour of the Callu version shipped with this distribution. The operator may also have their own privacy notice on top of this one.

teams.detail.roleSelectMemberDesc: Participate in on-call rotations and respond to incidents.
teams.detail.roleSelectObserverDesc: View-only access to team incidents and notifications.
maintenanceWindows.noSelectionHint: No selection = applies to all services.
reports.description: Analytics and insights for incident management
reports.comingSoonDesc: Detailed analytics and reporting will be available here
runbooks.description: Standard operating procedures for incident response
runbooks.comingSoonDesc: Create and manage incident response playbooks
postmortems.description: Learn from incidents and improve your processes
postmortems.comingSoonDesc: Document and review incident root causes
notifications.noNotificationsDesc: You'll see them here when notifications arrive
conference.title: Conference Bridge
help.heading: What this page is for
help.dashboard.title: Dashboard
help.dashboard.summary: An at-a-glance view of your incident response: open incidents, response metrics (MTTA/MTTR), recent activity and the health of your services.
help.dashboard.steps:
Use the time-range pills to scope the metrics to a period.
Click any recent incident to open its detail.
Watch the System Health panel for degraded or down services.
Jump to Teams or Services from the header buttons.
help.dashboard.tip: MTTA/MTTR update automatically as incidents are acknowledged and resolved.
help.incidents.title: Incidents
help.incidents.summary: Every alert that became an incident, with its status, severity and the affected service. The list updates in real time as incidents are created and change.
help.incidents.steps:
Filter by status or severity, or use the quick-filter buttons (Open, Critical, High).
Search incidents by title.
Click an incident to open its timeline and available actions.
Switch between card and table view and adjust the page size.
Select several with the checkboxes, then acknowledge or resolve them together from the action bar.
help.incidents.tip: Incidents are created automatically from inbound alerts/webhooks, or you can raise one manually.
help.incidentDetail.title: Incident detail
help.incidentDetail.summary: The full record of one incident — timeline, notes, who was paged, and the actions you can take to drive it to resolution.
help.incidentDetail.steps:
Acknowledge to stop escalation and signal you are on it.
Use Escalate to page the next step immediately when you need more help.
Resolve when it is fixed; Close later to archive it, or Reopen if it recurs.
Add notes to record what you find as you go.
Open or join a video conference to coordinate responders live.
help.incidentDetail.tip: Acknowledging halts the escalation timer so nobody else gets paged.
help.conferences.title: Conferences
help.conferences.summary: A read-only view of active and past video war-room conferences for your incidents, with recordings.
help.conferences.steps:
Filter conferences by status (Active, Ended, Expired).
Review each conference: incident, start time, participant count and recording.
Click Watch to play back a recording.
Click the incident link to open it — you join or open conferences from the incident detail page.
help.conferences.tip: You open and join conferences from an incident's detail page, not here.
help.services.title: Services
help.services.summary: The systems you monitor. Incidents attach to a service, and each service has its own inbound webhook, dependencies and acknowledgment callbacks.
help.services.steps:
Create a service with a name, type, environment and owning team.
Open it and go to the Webhooks tab to get the endpoint URL and generate an API key (optionally set up HMAC signing).
Turn on Listening Mode to receive payloads, then open Webhook Captures to inspect them.
Configure acknowledgment callbacks in ACK Settings, or manage Dependencies.
help.services.tip: Each service has its own webhook endpoint — that is how alerts turn into incidents.
help.escalations.title: Escalation policies
help.escalations.summary: The rules that decide who gets paged, in what order, and after how long an incident goes unacknowledged.
help.escalations.steps:
Create a policy, give it a name and assign an owner team (required).
Add steps with Add Step; the first step fires immediately, later steps use a delay you set.
For each step pick a target — a schedule's on-call, a team, or specific users.
Optionally enable per-step options like paging all team members or both on-call slots.
Save the policy, then attach it to a service (on the Services page) to route its incidents.
Use Run Simulation to preview the flow before saving.
help.escalations.tip: A step that targets a schedule pages whoever is on call right now, so paging follows your rotation automatically.
help.schedules.title: On-call schedules
help.schedules.summary: Timezone-aware rotations that determine who is on call at any given moment.
help.schedules.steps:
Create a schedule with a name, team and timezone.
On the Configuration tab set the rotation type (Daily/Weekly/Custom) and shift times.
On the Members tab add the people who rotate and order them.
Use the Preview tab to check who is on call before saving.
From the schedule list, add an Override when someone needs to cover a shift.
help.schedules.tip: Shifts are wall-clock, so daylight-saving changes are handled for you.
help.teams.title: Teams
help.teams.summary: Groups of people that own services, schedules and incidents.
help.teams.steps:
Create a team — enter a name, optional description and a color.
Add members from the available users and give each a role (Lead, Member or Observer).
Change roles or remove members from the team's detail view.
Note: services are assigned to a team from the Services page, not here.
help.teams.tip: A user must be on a team before they can be added to that team's on-call rotation.
help.reports.title: Reports
help.reports.summary: Trends and performance analytics across your incidents and services.
help.reports.steps:
Pick a date range.
Review incident trends, MTTA/MTTR, severity distribution and team performance.
Use service uptime to spot unreliable systems.
help.reports.tip: Use these to find recurring pain and to measure whether your response is improving over time.
help.postmortems.title: Postmortems
help.postmortems.summary: Structured write-ups of what happened, why, and the follow-ups — typically one per significant incident.
help.postmortems.steps:
Create a postmortem and link it to an incident.
Fill in the root cause and write the timeline in the Content field (Markdown).
Add action items with completion checkboxes.
Submit for review, then Publish — or Reject back to Draft if it needs more work.
Lock it when final to freeze the record.
help.postmortems.tip: Flow: Draft, In review, Published, Locked. A reviewer can reject from In review back to Draft.
help.runbooks.title: Runbooks
help.runbooks.summary: Reusable operational playbooks responders can follow step by step during an incident.
help.runbooks.steps:
Click New Runbook to create one.
Fill in the title, description and Markdown content with your procedure.
Add tags to categorize it.
Save it, then open it during an incident and press Play to record that it was used.
help.runbooks.tip: Keep runbooks short and action-oriented — they are read under pressure.
help.callLogs.title: Call logs
help.callLogs.summary: A record of every outbound voice call placed to page responders.
help.callLogs.steps:
Filter by call status (Acknowledged, Escalated, Connected, Failed, No Answer, Voicemail, Timeout), or search by recipient, phone or incident.
Click a row to see details: duration, attempt number, times and any failure reason.
Open the related incident from the detail view.
Check the Attempt column to spot retries and the status to find calls that did not connect.
help.callLogs.tip: Voice paging requires a configured voice provider in Communications.
help.maintenance.title: Maintenance windows
help.maintenance.summary: Planned windows that either suppress alerts or auto-acknowledge incidents for selected services.
help.maintenance.steps:
Click New Window and give it a title and time range.
Choose a mode: Suppress Alerts (silence notifications) or Auto-acknowledge (incidents are created but auto-acked).
Select the affected services, or tick Apply to All Services.
Create the window. Cancel a window early or Delete it from the list.
help.maintenance.tip: Suppressed alerts page nobody — scope the window tightly so you don't miss real problems.
help.settings.title: Settings
help.settings.summary: Workspace-wide configuration, organized into tabs: general details, email (SMTP), alert rules, notification channels, API keys and a danger zone.
help.settings.steps:
On General, set the org name, timezone, language and public base URL.
On Email, configure SMTP and send a test.
On Alert Rules, create rules that act on incoming alerts.
On Notifications, add channels (Slack, Teams, Email, Webhook) and test delivery.
On API Keys, review the per-service webhook keys.
help.settings.tip: The base URL is used to build links inside outgoing emails — set it correctly.
help.users.title: User management
help.users.summary: Invite people, control their access through roles, and manage the user list.
help.users.steps:
Click Invite User and choose an email and role (Admin, Team Lead, Member, Viewer).
Change a user's role anytime from the role dropdown in the table or the Edit modal.
Search and filter by name, email, role or status (Active/Pending).
Resend an invitation to a pending user with the mail icon.
Remove a user with the trash icon (not available for Admins).
help.users.tip: Invited users receive an email link to set their own password.
help.communications.title: Communications
help.communications.summary: Voice and SMS infrastructure: communication providers (Voximplant, Verimor), SIP trunks, and text-to-speech message templates by language.
help.communications.steps:
Add a provider (Voximplant for voice/video, Verimor for SMS) and enter its credentials.
Add a SIP trunk if your voice routing needs one.
Customize text-to-speech message text per language under TTS templates.
Activate a provider to use it for paging; the Last tested time shows its connectivity.
help.communications.tip: Chat channels (Slack, Teams, Webhook) live under Settings, Notifications tab — not here.
help.emailTemplates.title: Email templates
help.emailTemplates.summary: Customize the wording of the emails the app sends — invitations, alerts and notifications.
help.emailTemplates.steps:
Pick a template from the left.
Edit the subject and the body (HTML or Plain Text tabs).
Use the Preview tab to see how it renders, and the variables card to insert placeholders.
Send a test email to yourself, then Save Changes.
help.emailTemplates.tip: Keep the placeholders intact so the app can fill in incident details.
help.statusPage.title: Status page
help.statusPage.summary: A public page where customers can see your services' health and subscribe to updates.
help.statusPage.steps:
Pick which services appear and set each one's status (manually or via health checks).
Toggle the page public and set the company name and support email.
Copy and share the public URL; preview it before sharing.
Post incidents and maintenance to keep subscribers informed, and review email subscribers.
help.statusPage.tip: The public page needs no login, and only the services you select are shown.
help.notifications.title: Notifications
help.notifications.steps:
Filter by All, Unread or Read using the tabs.
Mark one as read with its check icon, or use Mark All As Read.
Click the link icon on a notification to open its incident (when it has one).
Manage your delivery preferences (Email, SMS, Voice, Push) from Settings.
help.notifications.tip: The bell in the header shows your unread in-app and email notifications.
help.profile.title: Profile
help.profile.summary: Your account details, contact information and notification preferences.
help.profile.steps:
Update your name, phone number and timezone in Personal Information.
Change your password in the Security section.
Turn email, SMS, voice and push notifications on or off in Notification Preferences, then save.
help.profile.tip: Add a phone number and enable SMS or Voice to be paged by phone during incidents.
profile.quietHoursDesc: Mute non-urgent notifications during these hours. Escalation pages always get through.
statusPage.slugDesc: Used in the public URL (/status/<slug>). Changing it breaks existing links to this page.
